items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution in Adobe Commerce Webhooks
items.summary: This critical vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source's webhooks component allows unauthenticated, zero-interaction arbitrary code execution due to improper output encoding. Adobe has released security updates to address this flaw.

items.title: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source allows an unrestricted upload of dangerous file types, which could lead to privilege escalation and arbitrary code execution. Exploitation requires user interaction.

items.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This high-severity stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source can lead to privilege escalation by injecting malicious scripts into the admin backend. It allows an attacker to gain control over an administrator's session.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization Vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Two high-severity incorrect authorization flaws in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source (CVE-2026-47988 and CVE-2026-47984) can circumvent default system rules and grant unauthorized access to resources. These issues were addressed in the APSB26-73 update.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension. A crafted cookie can lead to remote code execution without authentication or admin session. Mirasvit released version 1.11.12 to fix this flaw.

items.title: Denial-of-Service Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This critical denial-of-service vulnerability in Adobe Commerce is caused by a dependency on a vulnerable third-party component. It can be triggered remotely without authentication, leading to application crashes and disruption of storefront availability.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images. While Magento 2.4.9 contains a fix, older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This security update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read. Adobe recommends applying the patches as soon as possible.
